note: Mid-week manual (workflow_dispatch) checkpoint. Real ISO week W28 is 2026-07-06..07-12; only Mon-Wed elapsed. The sibling history/2026-W28.json is the established BASELINE but its window (2026-06-30..07-07) is mostly W27 — it is misnamed. This file captures the first robust full-day of true-W28 merge-group data (07-08) without overwriting that baseline. The complete W28 file should be written on the next full-week run and the naming collision resolved then.

runCount.note: 1 further run (14:56Z) still in-progress at analysis time, excluded

runnerWaitMed.note: per-run perceived minus exec on the same run; consistent low wait

comparisonToBaseline.webRunTestsStep: 07-08 median 114s vs baseline week-median 86s (+33%), BUT baseline late-day single-samples were 07-06=130s, 07-07=151s; 07-08 (7-run) at 114s is BELOW the late-week single-samples. The intra-week rise flagged in the baseline did NOT worsen — it stabilized/receded. No confirmed regression.
comparisonToBaseline.e2e: 07-08 median 214s vs baseline sampled 232s — slightly down.
comparisonToBaseline.worker: 07-08 median 99s vs baseline 80s — up ~24%, within single-day sample variance; watch.

flakyNote: Zero retries in sampled 07-08 logs: tests-worker (node24,pg12,mode) job 85899181216 and tests-web client project job 85899181223 both show cleanup immediately after the slowest-files block (the reporter prints 'Retried tests (N):' only when >=1 retry). Consistent with the baseline week's zero-flaky finding.

action.reason: Partial mid-week checkpoint: no regression (07-08 flat-to-down vs baseline), zero flaky, and the top deferred optimization candidate (webhooks.test.ts) was investigated and found unfit for an autonomous PR (see notes).
